title: grevillea juncifolia subsp. juncifolia
intro: Grevillea juncifolia subsp. juncifolia, also known as the emu bush, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the protea family. Traditionally, the leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Grevillea juncifolia subsp. juncifolia include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from pain and discomfort. The leaves can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture for medicinal purposes.
